step_by_step_thinking:
Accessory canals in mandibular first molars show considerable variability. While the majority of mandibular first molars possess distinct mesial and distal roots, they can demonstrate patterns beyond just these primary roots, particularly concerning lateral extensions and involvement in the furcation regions. Thus, option **\boxed{C}** suggesting the coexistence of both lateral and furcal canals aligns best with current knowledge about mandibular first molar anatomy.
The evidence indicates that while many mandibular first molars feature standard dual-root configurations, the existence of accessory canals extending laterally towards furcation areas adds important nuance to understanding potential complications during treatment. Therefore, considering the mixed nature of these anatomical findings,
